prompt: Play this short three-step World 8 ICM synthesis chain.

steps.prompt: Flop: Medium tournament pressure and a live stack should keep the line controlled. Choose action.
steps.feedback_correct_v1: Correct. This medium-pressure flop step prefers call.
steps.feedback_incorrect_v1: Incorrect. With medium tournament pressure still leaving room to maneuver, this flop should stay on the controlled call instead of escalating early.
steps.why_v1: When tournament pressure is present but not yet forcing collapse, controlled continuation preserves flexibility better than early escalation.

steps.prompt: Turn: Covering pressure and risk-premium leverage now support a stronger line. Choose action.
steps.feedback_correct_v1: Correct. This leverage-heavy turn step supports raise.
steps.feedback_incorrect_v1: Incorrect. Once covering pressure and risk-premium leverage both swing in your favor, this turn should press with a raise.
steps.why_v1: When the learner is applying pressure while covering the opponent, tournament leverage can justify a stronger line than raw chip EV alone.

steps.prompt: River: Covered pressure and tournament survival cost should close the line. Choose action.
steps.feedback_correct_v1: Correct. This covered river step should fold.
steps.feedback_incorrect_v1: Incorrect. When tournament survival cost dominates and you are the one under the covered pressure, this river should fold instead of paying off thinly.
steps.why_v1: When survival cost dominates and the learner is the one under tournament pressure, disciplined release is cleaner than forcing a thin continue.
